name: Forsyth County v. Nationalist Movement
question: Does an ordinance that permits a government administrator to vary the fee for assembling or parading to reflect the estimated cost of maintaining public order violate the free speech guarantees under the First and Fourteenth Amendments?
description: A case in which the Court that an ordinance that permits a government administrator to vary the fee for assembling or parading to reflect the estimated cost of maintaining public order violated the free-speech guarantees under the First and Fourteenth Amendments.
